mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near historic lows signals an accommodative monetary stance that reduces the equity discount rate and encourages risk-taking, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as long as the low-rate regime persists.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns during low-rate periods are not statistically higher than the full-sample mean (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises above a 0.3 percentage point increase over the trailing 3 months, this signals labor market deterioration that predicts near-term equity weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors price in slowing consumption and earnings risk.
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger fires, SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC is not significantly different from zero (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in PAYEMS growth and INDPRO growth indicates a synchronized labor-and-production slowdown that markets underprice individually; the joint signal produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days, whereas either series alone shows weak predictive power due to noise and revision risk.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth < 0.5% AND INDPRO 3-month annualized growth < 0%
failure_condition: The joint-trigger sample shows negative Rank IC not statistically distinguishable from the single-series baselines (delta IC < 0.02), or forward 60d returns are positive in majority of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) act as a leading indicator of broader demand only during periods of monetary tightening; a sustained decline in HOUST while FEDFUNDS is rising signals credit-sensitive demand contraction that precedes equity weakness over 20 trading days, but has no reliable signal value when rates are stable or falling.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines >5% over 3 months while FEDFUNDS is rising
failure_condition: During confirmed tightening episodes, HOUST decline fails to precede negative forward returns (win rate < 55%) or the effect disappears (Rank IC < 0.03) across at least two distinct tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeds a 3% threshold, elevated inflation compresses equity valuation multiples via expected policy tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days until inflation decelerates back below threshold.
failure_condition: In triggered high-inflation periods, forward 60d SPX returns are positive in over 55% of instances, or Rank IC is statistically indistinguishable from zero across two or more inflationary episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in RSAFS reflects resilient consumer spending that supports corporate revenue expectations, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days, active in all regimes as an always-on momentum proxy for consumption strength.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and forward 5d SPX returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or the sign of the relationship flips across more than one 3-year sub-period.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter contraction in GDPC1 signals an emerging recessionary regime that markets are slow to fully price, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days following the release of a negative GDP print, with effect strength contingent on confirmation from a subsequent print.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following a negative GDPC1 print are positive in over 55% of historical instances, or the effect is not robust across at least two distinct recession episodes.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of falling FEDFUNDS combined with rising UNRATE signals reactive monetary easing in response to labor market deterioration; this combination produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days because rate cuts in this context reflect deteriorating fundamentals rather than proactive stimulus, unlike easing during stable labor conditions.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls >0.25pp over 3 months AND UNRATE rises >0.2pp over same period
failure_condition: During joint-trigger episodes, SPX 20d forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction effect Rank IC does not exceed the single-series FEDFUNDS-only baseline by more than 0.02.
